data.title: HCA Healthcare Non-GAAP EPS of $7.15 in-line, revenue of $19.10B beats by $30M
data.description:
• HCA Healthcare press release (HCA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $7.15 in-line.
  • Revenue of $19.10B (+4.3% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 1.9 percent to $3.802 billion
  • Cash flows from operating activities increased 22.0 percent to $2.014 billion
  • Same facility admissions increased 0.9 percent and same facility equivalent admissions increased 1.3 percent

data.title: Charter Communications GAAP EPS of $9.17 misses by $0.91, revenue of $13.59B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Charter Communications press release (CHTR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $9.17 misses by $0.91.
  • Revenue of $13.59B (-1.1% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • First quarter revenue of $13.6 billion declined 1.0% year-over-year, primarily driven by lower residential video revenue. Residential connectivity revenue grew 0.9% year-over-year.
  • First quarter Spectrum Mobile™ lines increased by 368,000 and by 1.8 million over the last twelve months. As of March 31, 2026, Charter served 12.1 million mobile lines.
  • During the first quarter, Spectrum Internet® customers declined by 120,000. As of March 31, 2026, Charter served 29.6 million Internet customers.
  • As of March 31, 2026, customer relationships totaled 31.7 million and connectivity customers totaled 30.5 million.

data.title: AbbVie anti-wrinkle therapy rejected at FDA
data.description:
• The U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) has declined to approve TrenibotE (trenibotulinumtoxinE), an anti-wrinkle therapy developed by the AbbVie (ABBV) unit, Allergan Aesthetics.
  • In a statement late Thursday, AbbVie (ABBV) announced that the FDA issued a complete response letter in response to its Biologics License Application for TrenibotE, a botulinum neurotoxin with a rapid onset of action and a short duration of effect.
  • The Botox maker added that, according to the CRL, the FDA has not requested the company to conduct additional clinical studies or cited concerns over the safety or efficacy of TrenibotE. However, the regulator has sought additional information regarding manufacturing processes.
  • “AbbVie is confident that it can address the FDA's comments promptly and expects to submit a thorough response in the coming months,” the company said, adding that regulatory reviews for TrenibotE are currently underway in overseas markets as expected.

data.title: SLB Non-GAAP EPS of $0.52 in-line, revenue of $8.72B beats by $60M
data.description:
• SLB press release (SLB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.52 in-line.
  • Revenue of $8.72B (+2.7% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Board approved quarterly cash dividend of $0.295 per share
  • Shares -2% PM.

data.title: MaxLinear jumps after Q1 beat, Q2 outlook tops views on AI data center demand
data.description:
MaxLinear (MXL) shares jumped 38% in premarket trading on Friday after the chipmaker posted first-quarter results that topped estimates and issued a stronger-than-expected revenue forecast, on strong optical data-center business performance tied to AI infrastructure. 

The company on Thursday reported adjusted earnings of $0.22 a share for the quarter ended March, beating consensus by $0.4. Revenue rose 43% from a year earlier to $137.2M, also ahead of analyst expectations by $2.62M.

"Q1 was a strong and important start to the year, and we believe it marks the beginning of a multiyear growth phase for MaxLinear, led by our optical data center business," said CEO Kishore Seendripu in earnings call. 

MaxLinear forecasts revenue of $160M to $170M for Q2, which was well above Wall Street estimates of about $144M. "We are now at a clear inflection point in our optical data center business marked by a step function increase in revenues expected in Q2,”  said Seendripu

Management pointed to a ramp in its Keystone optical interconnect products, which are used in high-speed networking systems inside hyperscale data centers. 

MaxLinear raised its 2026 outlook for optical data-center revenue to $150M to $170M, up from a prior view of $100M to $130M. Seendripu said Keystone products are now ramping with multiple major hyperscale customers in the U.S. and Asia, supporting 400G and 800G deployments for AI scale-up and scale-out workloads. 

Executives said industry supply constraints persist and input costs for wafers and packaging remain elevated, while the company used cash during the quarter partly for wafer prepayments to secure capacity for expected second-half demand.

data.title: Vladimir Galkin discloses an active stake in JetBlue and Spirit
data.description:
Vladimir Galkin disclosed in SEC filings that he holds a 10% active stake in JetBlue (JBLU) and a 5% active stake in Spirit Aviation Holdings (FLYQQ).

Galkin is believed to be JetBlue's (JBLU) second-largest investor, with his double-digit stake. He is well known as a big beneficiary of GameStop's (GME) meme stock rally in 2021, which he then funneled into JetBlue stock (JBLU) between February and August 2024. Galkin has met with JetBlue executives in the past, including CEO Joanna Geraghty and CFO Ursula Hurley, to discuss the possibility of joining the airline's board of directors.

Last year, Galkin threatened  to sell his large stake in JetBlue (JBLU) if the airline company did not turn around its financial performance through cutting costs and other measures. 

Yesterday, President Donald Trump suggested that the U.S. government may buy Spirit Airlines (FLYYQ), amid discussions for a possible rescue package for the troubled discount airline. Shares of Spirit Aviation (FLYYQ) ripped a gain of more than 350% for the week amid buyout speculation.

data.title: Boyd Gaming falls after Q profit miss, soft Las Vegas locals results
data.description:
Boyd Gaming (BYD) reported revenues rose 0.6% year over year to $997.4M in Q1. Net income was $105.5M vs. $111.4M a year ago. Total adjusted EBITDAR was $317.4M vs. $337.5M a year ago. Non-GAAP EPS of $1.60 was below the consensus estimate of $1.71 and below last year's mark of $1.62.

The company noted that its Midwest & South segment saw revenue and adjusted EBITDAR growth, driven by continued growth in play from core and retail players, favorable comparisons due to last year's severe winter weather, and contributions from recent property investments. Results in the Las Vegas Locals segment were impacted by continued softness in destination business, as well as construction disruption from the ongoing renovations at Suncoast. During the quarter, results in the Downtown Las Vegas segment were noted to reflect stability in play from Hawaiian guests and reduced destination business.

At the end of the quarter, Boyd Gaming (BYD) had a cash position of $372.7M, and total debt of $2.3B.

"Looking ahead, we believe that our strong balance sheet, diversified portfolio, balanced approach to capital allocation, and experienced management team all position us well to continue creating long-term value for our shareholders," highlighted CEO Keith Smith.

Shares of Boyd Gaming (BYD) fell 5.8% in premarket trading on Friday.

data.title: Apogee Non-GAAP EPS of $0.92 beats by $0.06, revenue of $351.35M beats by $15.89M
data.description:
• Apogee press release (APOG): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.92 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $351.35M (+1.6% Y/Y) beats by $15.89M.
  • Net sales increased 1.6% to $351.4 million, driven by favorable price and mix, partially offset by lower volume.
  • Based on current macroeconomic conditions, the Company expects net sales to be in the range of $1.38 billion to $1.43 billion vs $1.39B consensus, and adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $2.70 to $3.25 vs $3.43 consensus. The Company’s outlook assumes interest expense of approximately $10 million, an adjusted effective tax rate of 26% to 27%, and capital expenditures between $35 million to $40 million.

data.title: EU to move forward with negotiations over Ukraine's membership - report
data.description:
European Union leaders at a summit in Cyprus agreed that the first negotiations for Ukraine to join the bloc could start in the coming weeks and months, Bloomberg News reported, citing an EU official.

The progress follows Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's defeat in the parliamentary election this month. Hungary previously blocked Ukraine's path to joining the EU.

But there was no commitment on a date for Ukraine's accession. The membership process for Croatia, the last country to join the EU, took about a decade.

Ahead of the Cyprus summit, France and Germany reportedly pitched the idea of an "associate membership" option for Ukraine. Under this plan, Ukraine would integrate into EU programs gradually and attend the bloc's meetings, but wouldn't have voting rights. 

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy rejected the idea. "Ukraine does not need symbolic membership in the EU," he said. "Ukraine is defending itself and is definitely defending Europe. It is not defending Europe symbolically."

Estonia backs Ukraine's full membership in the EU, Prime Minister Kristen Michal told Bloomberg in an interview, "because, to be honest, there's no other way."

The renewed push for Ukraine's EU membership comes as the bloc finalized a €90B ($105.18B) loan to the country after Hungary lifted its veto. The loan, which will help cover Ukraine's budgetary and defense industrial capacity needs in 2026 and 2027, is to be repaid by Russia's reparations to Ukraine.
